payload.answer: mem_new was returned because it is the active memory that matches Sam and Goldman. mem_old was filtered because it is superseded, so the recall system suppresses it rather than showing an outdated version as current.
payload.decision: Recall returned mem_new as the current matching memory for Sam and Goldman, and filtered out mem_old because mem_old is marked as superseded and therefore should not be presented as current.

entity_resolution.reason: The explanation depends on the matched entities Sam and Goldman, but no separate canonical entity ID is provided in the fixture.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the active memory matching the Sam + Goldman query and is treated as the current version.
mem_old also matched Sam and Goldman, but it was excluded because it has been superseded by a newer memory and should not be surfaced as current.

details: Preserved ambiguity about the exact underlying fact and did not restate the superseded content as current.

repair_options: Verify that mem_old has an explicit superseded-by link to mem_new.
Check whether mem_new and mem_old are attached to the same Sam and Goldman entities.
If mem_old should still be recallable, change its status from superseded to historical instead of suppressing it.
Inspect ranking/debug traces to confirm supersession filtering happened after both memories matched the query.
